lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you Sir.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well hello my brave adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Getting ready to go fishing of course. There's no time to waste!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I've got all the supplies I need from the shop at the end of the pier. They sell good rope, hammers and axes although their bailing buckets aren't too effective.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What fish do you catch?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I get all sorts, anything that lies on the sea bed, you never know what you're going to get until you pull up the net!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your boat doesn't look too safe.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: That's because it's not, the darn thing's full of holes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so I suppose you can't go out for a while?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Oh no, I don't let a few holes stop an experienced sailor like me. I could sail these seas in a barrel, I'll be going out soon enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I help?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well of course you can! I'll warn you though, the seas are merciless and without fishing experience you won't catch much.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You need a fishing level of 15 or above to catch any fish on the trawler.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: On occasions the net rips, so you'll need some rope to repair it.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Repairing the net is difficult in the harsh conditions so you'll find it easier with a higher crafting level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...ok.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: There's also a slight problem with leaks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leaks?!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Nothing some swamp paste won't fix...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Swamp paste?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Also, there's the small matter of the Kraken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have got to be kidding me?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Occupational hazard. I usually whack it with an axe a few time and fix the damage with hammer once it's gone.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Oh, and one more thing... I hope you're a good swimmer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'll leave it.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Bloomin' land lovers!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be fine, let's go.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Aye aye! Meet me on board the trawler. I just need to get a few things together.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Aye aye! Meet me on board the trawler.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Oh hang on. I'm already on a fishing trip with someone. You'll have to try back in a few minutes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you be here and on board the boat?!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: That's Game Magic, [lass/son].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Never mind. Just try back later OK?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's swamp paste?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Swamp tar mixed with flour which is then heated over a fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find swamp tar?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Unfortunately the only supply of swamp tar is in the swamps below Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Murphy.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Good day to you land lover. Fancy hitting the high seas again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I still feel ill from last time.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Hah! ...Softy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We keep sinking! Can you give me some advice?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Aye aye matey! We'll soon get you ship shape!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You need a fishing level of 15 or above to catch any fish on the trawler.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: On occasions the net rips, so you'll need some rope to repair it.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Repairing the net is difficult in the harsh conditions so you'll find it easier with a higher crafting level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, got it.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: There's also a slight problem with leaks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't say!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Nothing some swamp paste won't fix...

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Also, bring a good axe and a hammer in case of Kraken attacks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I'd heard it all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right so I need lots of swamp paste and plenty of rope, an axe and a hammer. Thanks Murphy!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: If all else fails use a bailing bucket or three!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm afraid my setup is a bit dated these days. That said, I've heard there's a retired netmaster over in Port Roberts who's more than happy to share his trawling secrets. Kellan I think his name was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I think I've met him. I'll have to go and have another word with him about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... I might have to sail over there and introduce myself.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You won't be able to trawl without a boat of your own. Perhaps someone over at Port Sarim could help you with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's do it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Murphy, what can you tell me about giant crabs?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Giant Crabs? Why, I used to haul up a few of those whenever I set my nets around Rimmington.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Why the interest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get some Giant Crab Meat and Kelp. Can you tell me where I can get some?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, the only thing I can think to do is to go down there and look to see if there are any left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go down there? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: As in under the water!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Tell me, can you get your hands on a fishbowl?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I will be able to if I put my mind to it, why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, I know how to rig up a handy set of breathing gear that can allow you to keep air inside the fishbowl.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You just put the barrel of air on your back, with the fishbowl over your head, and then dive over the side.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: It should hold enough air to keep you alive for quite some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds pretty dangerous...

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Not at all. I'll even weigh anchor with the chain somewhere easy to reach so you can climb out easily enough if you run into trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still not happy about this...

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, I can't think of any other way to check. If you come up with a better plan then feel free to tell me.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: In the meantime I'll wait, and if you want me to take you diving I'll be ready.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: By the way, why are you so interested in crabs all of a sudden?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't tell you I'm afraid. The very fabric of space and time could collapse!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, I guess some people will do anything for a bit of fresh seafood.

sections.title: Talking to Murphy again with a fish bowl

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ahoy there! All set for a dive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I certainly am.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Great! I'll sort that fishbowl out for you.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure this is safe?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Sure! It's as sound as my ship!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Want me to take you out now?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well I can't take you out until you are properly geared up.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok, let's be off then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, see you soon Murphy!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Good luck, I'll weigh anchor. Climb up the chain to get aboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just yet.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok, well I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ahoy there! All set for a dive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Murphy, can I get another diving helmet?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Sure, here you go. Try not to lose this one, I'm not a very good glassblower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, the bowls I make tend to shatter under pressure into razor-sharp shards of lethal, face shredding shrapnel.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Don't let it bother you, I think I know what I'm doing wrong now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Murphy. Do you have a spare diving backpack?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Sure, I have plenty after the...little incident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What 'little incident'?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Nothing to worry yourself about.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Here, have this one. Only dropped once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that blood?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: No, no.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, yes.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: But seriously, what are the chances of getting a jellyfish jammed in there again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well let's be...

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Err, how do you plan on swimming with your hands full?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, when you hit the water you will start swimming. If you can't free up your hands by putting everything away then you will sink like a stone.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: All you have to do is have 2 spaces to store the items and you are good to go.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Even if you aren't carrying a shield or weapon it's always handy to have some free space for an emergency.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: See you then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Now, do you have a gomeball on you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do, why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well you will need to leave them on the surface. They are so full of air that they will stop you diving properly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll go put it in the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Now, do you have a hand egg on you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do, why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well you will need to leave them on the surface. They are so full of air that they will stop you diving properly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll go put it in the bank.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You want that cat of yours to drown or something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you can't make them an adorable set of tiny diving gear?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: No. Just...no.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm not taking you out for a dive with that cat following you.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm not a cat person, and it'd have to stay on the ship while you were down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You know, I can see your cat right there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm looking right at it. See, it's just there in your bag!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm not taking you out with that cat. I'm not some sort of cat-sitter, so go and put it somewhere safe, then come back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: O'kay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still think I should get some cat-sized diving gear…

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm not taking you out for a dive with that pet following you. It'll get wet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I'm not taking you out for a dive with that pet in your bag. It'll get wet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well I can't take you out until you are properly geared up.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We lost three diving parties that way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, well I'll just put my diving helmet and diving backpack on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well that's the sort of spirit I like to see. However you are far too heavy to go diving right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, I can't take you diving if you are carrying more than 27kg worth of equipment.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We arrived at that number through trial and error, and the errors were not pretty let me assure you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can I do?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, if you pop over to a bank and leave all your heavy equipment behind then I'll take you out.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: See you then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Let's go diving.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok, let's be off then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, see you soon Murphy!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Good luck, I'll weigh anchor. Climb up the chain to get aboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just yet.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok, well I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Murphy.

sections.title: When climbing back up the anchor

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: How did it go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Great, I'll take us back in.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship's ready to depart.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: By the way, would you like me to remind you how the trawler works once the trip starts?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: By the way, would you like me to keep reminding you how the trawler works once the trip starts?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You've got it!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: No worries! Let me know if you change your mind!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship's at sea at the moment. This is some kind of paradox.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Whoooahh sailor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had enough, take me back.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Hah! .. the soft land lovers lost their sea legs have they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Something like that.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We're too far out now, It'd be dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I insist Murphy, take me back.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok, ok, I'll try, but don't say I didn't warn you…

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Save yourself! We're going down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then Murphy, just keep us afloat.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: That's the attitude sailor...Aaaaar!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How you doing Murphy?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Get those fishies!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I think i'm feeling a bit sea sick!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: It's a fierce sea today traveller, you best hold on tight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahoy there!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ahoy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to learn how to be a navigator.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well, you've come to the right place, m'hearty! What do you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The professor said that I need to have a sextant. Do you know where I can get one?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Hmm. I used to use a sextant when I was a young fella.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you still have it?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have it?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you still need it?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: I can tell from the taste of the sea spray where I am, m'hearty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: What the...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, Murphy. My name's [player name] and I'm using magic to contact you!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: That's so rude when you may as well just speak to me in person!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Well I never! Technology these days. Beyond me. So how might I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just wondering if there is anyone down there helping you out on the trawler?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The boat has just left actually! Perhaps when it comes back you can join them in another run?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: There seems to be someone on-board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just one person?

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Yep, just one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, maybe I'll join them.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: No, there are [number] at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, maybe I'll join them.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The boat is just sitting waiting for someone to join. How about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I'll come have a look. I'll catch you later!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Ok mate, bye!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship is back in port!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will leave in 1 minute!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will leave in [50/40/30/20/10] seconds!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship has just left port!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will be back in [5/4/3/2/1] minute[s]!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will be back in under 1 minute!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Looks like the ship’s not going after all!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship has sunk! I'll get another one.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will leave in 1 minute!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The ship will leave in [50/40/30/20/10] seconds!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Let’s get this net full with fishies!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Arrh! Check that net!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Doom! The Kraken is attacking!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Hurrah! The Kraken is no more!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You’ll need to start bailing soon!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Blistering barnacles!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: The water is coming in matey!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We’ll all end up in a watery grave!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Land lubbers!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: It’s a fierce sea today traveller.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: My mother could bail better than that!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Noooooo!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We’re going under!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: We’re all doomed!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Mummy!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You’ll need all your strength!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: To make it back to shore.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: No fishies for you today!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Arrrgh! We’ve sunk!

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: Keep your head above water shipmate.

lines.speaker: Murphy
lines.text: You’ll be joining Davey Jones!
